methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: mintPrice calculation based on generation mint count and priceIncrement
data_flows.trust_changes: ETH transitions from user-controlled to protocol-controlled upon mint; NukeFund receive() trusts any sender
exposure_risks: NukeFund receive() is public, anyone can send ETH and trigger dev share distribution
manipulation_risks: If taxCut is set to 0 by owner, division by zero in NukeFund.receive()
If taxCut is set to 1, 100% goes to devShare and 0% to fund

path: writeEntropyBatch1/2/3 generates pseudo-random values from block.number and index
getNextEntropy() called by TraitForgeNft during mint
getEntropy() extracts 6-digit value from slot via position slicing
sinks: Used for role determination, forge potential, nuke factor, performance factor
data_flows.trust_changes: Entropy generated from miner-influenceable block data; once stored, treated as immutable truth for game mechanics
exposure_risks: getPublicEntropy is public view - all future entropies are predictable once slots are written
entropySlots can be read via storage slots directly
manipulation_risks: Miners can influence block.number to manipulate entropy values
All batches written in same block get identical block.number input - values vary only by index i
Users can predict next entropy and front-run or time mints for desirable traits
writeEntropyBatch3 doesn't check for 999999, allowing the special alpha value to be placed incorrectly

data_flows.source: User calls forgeWithListed with forgerTokenId and mergerTokenId
path: Validates forger is listed, caller owns merger, different owners, same generation
Resets forging counts if year elapsed
Calls nftContract.forge() to mint new entity
ETH split: devFee to nukeFund, forgerShare to forger owner
transformations: Fee split: forgingFee/taxCut to nukeFund, remainder to forger owner
sinks: New token minted to merger owner
ETH to nukeFund and forger owner
data_flows.trust_changes: Forger owner address read from nftContract.ownerOf at time of forge - could change between listing and forging if not properly guarded
exposure_risks: Forger's forge count not validated against forgePotential in forgeWithListed - only checked during listForForging
manipulation_risks: Race condition: forger lists, year resets count to 0, forger can forge more times than forgePotential if listing persists across year boundary
Excess ETH sent to forgeWithListed is not refunded

path: Validates contract is approved for transfer
claimAmount = min(fund * nukeFactor / MAX_DENOMINATOR, fund / maxAllowedClaimDivisor)
transformations: Age calculation: daysOld * performanceFactor * MAX_DENOMINATOR * ageMultiplier / 365
ClaimAmount: fund * finalNukeFactor / MAX_DENOMINATOR capped at fund/maxAllowedClaimDivisor
data_flows.trust_changes: Fund balance decremented before ETH transfer (no reentrancy issue due to nonReentrant), but burn calls back to TraitForgeNft which may have side effects
exposure_risks: If ageMultiplier is 0 (uninitialized), adjustedAge is always 0, nuke factor is only entropy/40
manipulation_risks: If ageMultiplier is not set, calculateAge returns 0, making nuke factor purely entropy-based and ignoring age
Token with entropy 999999 gives initialNukeFactor = 999999/40 = 24999, which is near the 50000 cap, allowing near-max claims quickly
lastTokenTransferredTimestamp is 0 for tokens never transferred (only minted), so canTokenBeNuked uses creation-era timestamp making minimumDaysHeld trivially met for old minted tokens

transformations: Tax split: msg.value/taxCut to nukeFund, remainder to seller
data_flows.trust_changes: ETH flows to seller address stored in listing - if seller is a contract, re-entrancy is possible within the nonReentrant scope
exposure_risks: listing.isActive not checked in buyNFT - only seller and price checked
manipulation_risks: After buyNFT deletes the listing, listedTokenIds[tokenId] still points to the old listingCount index - stale mapping
The _beforeTokenTransfer hook in TraitForgeNft will try to cancel forging listing when NFT is transferred from EntityTrading to buyer

data_flows.source: ETH sent to DevFund via receive()
path: Devs call claim() to collect pending
transformations: Integer division truncation: dust goes to owner
data_flows.trust_changes: ETH held by contract until claimed; balance must cover all pending rewards
exposure_risks: If a dev is removed but doesn't claim, their pendingRewards are stored but weight is 0 - they can still claim
manipulation_risks: Owner can add/update/remove devs at any time, potentially diluting or stealing unclaimed rewards
No timelock or governance on dev weight changes

transformations: Hash-based pseudo-random selection of special 999999 entropy position
data_flows.trust_changes: Miner-influenceable block data determines which token gets the 999999 golden entropy
validation_points: onlyOwner modifier - but called from _incrementGeneration in TraitForgeNft which is not owner
exposure_risks: initializeAlphaIndices is onlyOwner but TraitForgeNft calls it in _incrementGeneration - this will REVERT unless TraitForgeNft is owner of EntropyGenerator
manipulation_risks: Miner can manipulate blockhash to influence alpha placement
If slots 512-769 haven't been initialized yet (batch3 not called), the alpha position maps to uninitialized slot

data_flows.source: Any ERC721 transfer (including mint, burn, trade)
path: If listed, fetches listing details and cancels if conditions match
transformations: Timestamp update on transfer for nuke maturity tracking
data_flows.trust_changes: TraitForgeNft calls entityForgingContract.cancelListingForForging - requires TraitForgeNft to be authorized
validation_points: from != to check for timestamp update
exposure_risks: On mint (from=address(0)), lastTokenTransferredTimestamp is not set since from!=to is true but transfer is from zero address
manipulation_risks: lastTokenTransferredTimestamp is set on mint via _beforeTokenTransfer (from=0, to=user), but canTokenBeNuked in NukeFund checks getTokenLastTransferredTimestamp which returns lastTokenTransferredTimestamp - if this is set to current timestamp on mint, the minimumDaysHeld works correctly. But if token is never transferred after mint, this timestamp captures mint time.

core_rules: Max 10000 tokens per generation, max 10 generations = 100000 total tokens
Tokens are either forgers (entropy % 3 == 0) or mergers (entropy % 3 != 0)
Forge potential determined by 5th digit of entropy: (entropy / 10) % 10
Minting price increases linearly within a generation
Nuke fund accumulates 90% of all minting fees
Nuke payouts based on age and entropy-derived nuke factor
Forging requires same generation for both parents
New forged entity is one generation higher than parents
economic_incentives: Hold tokens longer for higher nuke factor (age-based)
Higher entropy tokens have higher initial nuke factor
Forgers earn fees from merger owners who want new entities
10% tax on all economic activities feeds the nuke fund
Developers receive weighted share of dev fund distributions
governance_mechanisms: Owner can change all critical parameters (taxCut, prices, addresses)
Owner controls dev fund weight allocation

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Age should meaningfully contribute to nuke factor
edge_cases.actual_behavior: calculateAge always returns 0 because daysOld * perfomanceFactor * MAX_DENOMINATOR * 0 / 365 = 0, making nuke factor purely entropy/40
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Tokens with high entropy (e.g., 999999) can be immediately nuked for high payouts without needing to hold them for extended periods, defeating the age-based incentive mechanism

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Alpha indices should be reinitialized each generation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: initializeAlphaIndices has onlyOwner modifier on EntropyGenerator. Unless TraitForgeNft is set as the owner of EntropyGenerator, this call will REVERT, preventing generation increments entirely
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Generation advancement is completely blocked. No new generations can be minted after the first 10000 tokens. This is a critical functional break. The onlyAllowedCaller modifier should have been used instead of onlyOwner.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: numberOfDigits(0) returns 0, causing paddedEntropy = 0 * 10^(6-0) = 0 * 10^6 = 0. However, the 10^6 exponent creates issues if entropy is 0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Token with 0 entropy has: isForger = (0%3==0) = true, forgePotential = (0/10)%10 = 0, performanceFactor = 0%10 = 0, nukeFactor = 0/40 = 0. ForgePotential of 0 means it can never forge despite being a forger. Performance factor of 0 means age calculation is always 0.

edge_cases.scenario: Forger's forge potential not rechecked in forgeWithListed
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Both forger and merger should be validated against their forge potential limits at time of forging
edge_cases.actual_behavior: forgingCounts[forgerTokenId]++ happens without checking against forgePotential. The check was only done at listing time, but year-based reset could have occurred
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A forger lists when forgingCounts < forgePotential. After listing, year passes and count resets to 0. Multiple forgeWithListed calls can be made, each incrementing the count. The forger can forge many more times than forgePotential because count was reset after the listing check.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Compares _tokenIds (global counter) against maxTokensPerGen (10000). After first generation, _tokenIds > 10000, so the loop condition fails and no more tokens can be minted via mintWithBudget
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: mintWithBudget becomes completely non-functional after generation 1 since _tokenIds will exceed 10000. Users can only use mintToken for generations 2+.
Owner setting taxCut to 0 would brick all minting, trading, and forging since fund distribution would revert. Same for EntityForging and EntityTrading.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: initialNukeFactor = 999999/40 = 24999. With nukeFactorMaxParam = 50000, the token could claim fund * 24999 / 100000 = ~25% of fund immediately (or up to 50% if age contributes). The alpha token is extremely valuable for nuking.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Predictable alpha position via getPublicEntropy. Attacker can calculate when 999999 will be minted and time their mint to capture it, then immediately nuke for ~25% of the fund.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: canTokenBeNuked should account for tokens that were minted but never transferred
edge_cases.actual_behavior: getTokenLastTransferredTimestamp returns lastTokenTransferredTimestamp which IS set during _beforeTokenTransfer on mint (from=address(0) to user, from != to so it gets set). So the timestamp is set to mint time.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No issue - timestamp is properly set on mint via _beforeTokenTransfer hook

edge_cases.scenario: Excess ETH in forgeWithListed not refunded
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Excess ETH above forgingFee should be refunded to caller
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only msg.value >= forgingFee is checked. Any excess ETH is trapped in the EntityForging contract forever.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Users lose excess ETH sent with forging transactions. The ETH is permanently locked in the EntityForging contract with no withdrawal function.

edge_cases.scenario: Forged entity entropy averaging can produce non-forger from two special tokens
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Forged entity properties should be well-defined
edge_cases.actual_behavior: newEntropy = (forgerEntropy + mergerEntropy) / 2 with integer division. The resulting token's role, forge potential, etc. are derived from this averaged entropy which may have unexpected properties
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Players can predict the resulting entropy and properties of forged entities, gaming the system for desired traits

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should verify the listing is active before allowing purchase
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only checks listing.seller != address(0) and msg.value == listing.price. After a listing is deleted, the default Listing has seller=address(0) and price=0, so sending 0 ETH would pass the price check but fail the seller check. However, there's no explicit isActive validation.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not directly exploitable due to seller != address(0) check, but missing isActive check is poor practice

edge_cases.scenario: cancelListingForForging does not delete listedTokenIds mapping
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Both listings and listedTokenIds should be cleaned up
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _cancelListingForForging only deletes listings[listedTokenIds[tokenId]] but does not delete listedTokenIds[tokenId]. The stale listedTokenIds mapping could cause getListedTokenIds to return a non-zero value for a cancelled listing.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: In _beforeTokenTransfer, getListedTokenIds returns stale value, but the subsequent check verifies listing details match. However, if a new listing occupies the same index, it could cause incorrect cancellation of a different token's listing.

edge_cases.scenario: writeEntropyBatch1 can be called again after batches are complete if lastInitializedIndex wraps
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each batch should only be writable once
edge_cases.actual_behavior: lastInitializedIndex starts at 0, batch1 sets it to 256, batch2 to 512, batch3 to 770. The require checks prevent re-calling. However, the entropy slots are never re-initialized between generations despite indices resetting via getNextEntropy slot wrap-around.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: After all 10010 entropies are used (770 slots * 13), getNextEntropy wraps currentSlotIndex to 0, reusing the same entropy values. All subsequent tokens across future generations have predictable, repeated entropy values.

edge_cases.scenario: NukeFund.nuke burns token AFTER calculating claim amount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Burning should not affect claim calculation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The burn calls _burn which triggers _beforeTokenTransfer which updates timestamps and potentially cancels forging listings. The burn also calls airdropContract.subUserAmount. If any of these external calls fail or have unexpected behavior, the nuke could revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If airdropContract is malicious or broken, nuke operations could be blocked, trapping funds

edge_cases.scenario: calculateMintPrice uses currentGeneration but _mintInternal may increment generation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Price should be consistent with the generation being minted in
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In mintWithBudget, calculateMintPrice is called in the while loop. _mintInternal can call _incrementGeneration which changes currentGeneration and priceIncrement. The next iteration's calculateMintPrice uses the new generation's count (0) and higher priceIncrement, potentially resulting in a price that equals startPrice (since count is 0) but with a higher base.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Price drops to startPrice when generation increments within mintWithBudget, allowing users to mint the first token of a new generation at a discount

edge_cases.scenario: DevFund totalRewardDebt accumulation with no devs then devs added
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Rewards should only accumulate for registered devs
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When totalDevWeight > 0, totalRewardDebt += amountPerWeight. If devs are added after funds arrive, they start with rewardDebt = totalRewardDebt, missing previous rewards. But if funds arrive when no devs exist, everything goes to owner. Correct behavior.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploit, but timing of dev registration matters

edge_cases.scenario: Forging count check is off-by-one: forgingCounts[forgerTokenId] <= forgePotential in listForForging
edge_cases.actual_behavior: forgingCounts starts at 0, check is forgingCounts <= forgePotential. With forgePotential=1: count=0 passes (0<=1), count=1 passes (1<=1), then forge increments to 2. Next listing check: 2<=1 fails. So token can forge twice with potential of 1. Should be forgingCounts < forgePotential.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Every forger can forge one more time than their forgePotential digit indicates. A forger with forgePotential=1 can forge twice, forgePotential=5 can forge 6 times, etc.

assumption_violations: EntropyGenerator.initializeAlphaIndices assumes it's called by the contract owner, but TraitForgeNft._incrementGeneration calls it - TraitForgeNft must be the owner of EntropyGenerator or this breaks entirely
mintWithBudget assumes _tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen is the correct loop termination but _tokenIds is a global counter that only increases, breaking after generation 1
NukeFund assumes ageMultiplier is set to a non-zero value; default 0 makes age meaningless

EntropyGenerator assumes 770 slots * 13 numbers = 10010 entropies are sufficient; with 10000 tokens per generation and up to 10 generations, that's potentially 100000+ tokens but only 10010 unique entropies before recycling
EntityForging assumes forge potential check at listing time is sufficient - does not recheck at forge time
The system assumes NukeFund, DevFund addresses are correctly set before minting begins; no validation that these are set
game_theory_risks: Entropy predictability: since getPublicEntropy is public view, sophisticated players can predict which mint will yield high-value tokens (especially 999999) and time their mints accordingly
Nuke fund drainage: With multiple high-entropy tokens, coordinated nuking can rapidly drain the fund. Each nuke takes up to 50% of remaining fund, so serial nuking geometrically reduces the fund
Forging arbitrage: forger sets high fee, merger pays fee, new token may be immediately nukeable for more than the forging fee if fund is large enough
Front-running: miners or MEV bots can observe mintToken transactions and front-run to claim desirable entropy values
Airdrop gaming: airdropContract.addUserAmount is called with entropy value, incentivizing users to mint high-entropy tokens before airdrop starts
Generation transition sniping: first token of new generation costs only startPrice since generationMintCounts resets to 0
integration_risks: NukeFund.receive() can revert if devAddress/daoAddress/owner is a contract that rejects ETH, bricking all minting
airdropContract external calls in _mintInternal and burn could revert, blocking minting and nuking
entityForgingContract.cancelListingForForging called from _beforeTokenTransfer could revert, blocking all token transfers
If entityForgingContract or entropyGenerator are not properly set, minting reverts
DevFund receive() sends remainder to owner - if owner is a contract that reverts on receive, all fund distribution is blocked
calculation_errors: Forging count off-by-one: forgingCounts[tokenId] <= forgePotential should be < forgePotential, allowing one extra forge per token
mintWithBudget loop condition uses _tokenIds instead of generationMintCounts[currentGeneration], breaking for generations > 1
numberOfDigits(0) returns 0, causing paddedEntropy = 0 * 10^6 which is 0 but the exponent 10^(6-0) = 10^6 is technically fine since 0 * anything = 0
calculateAge: if ageMultiplier is 0, the entire age calculation is 0 regardless of how long the token is held
entropy averaging in forge: (forgerEntropy + mergerEntropy) / 2 loses 1 bit of precision due to integer division, minor but could affect role determination
priceIncrement is permanently modified in _incrementGeneration (priceIncrement += priceIncrementByGen), affecting all future generations cumulatively

token_semantics.notes: ERC721Enumerable NFT. Minting restricted to internal functions and forge(). Burning restricted to approved/owner. Transfer hook cancels forging listings and updates timestamps. No fee-on-transfer.

Native ETH used for all value transfers. No ERC20 tokens in the protocol. ETH flows: minting fees -> NukeFund -> devAddress; forging fees -> nukeFund + forger; trading fees -> nukeFund + seller; nuke claims <- NukeFund.

invariants.name: fund_balance_conservation
invariants.description: NukeFund.fund should always be <= address(NukeFund).balance. The difference accounts for devShare portion that may not yet be distributed.

invariants.name: generation_mint_count_bound
invariants.description: generationMintCounts[gen] should never exceed maxTokensPerGen for any generation

invariants.name: token_generation_bound
invariants.description: No token should have a generation greater than maxGeneration

invariants.name: entropy_slot_bounds
invariants.description: currentSlotIndex should be < 770 and currentNumberIndex should be < 13

invariants.name: forging_count_bound
invariants.description: VIOLATED: forgingCounts can exceed forgePotential by 1 due to <= comparison instead of <

invariants.name: claim_amount_cap
invariants.description: claimAmount should never exceed fund / maxAllowedClaimDivisor (50% of fund by default)

invariants.name: dev_reward_solvency
invariants.description: Sum of all pending rewards should not exceed contract balance. safeRewardTransfer provides a soft cap.
